issue_title: Add type hinting
issue_body:
Now that ``cmd2`` no longer needs to support Python 2.7 from release 0.9.0 onward, we can begin to add [type hinting](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html) to function/method arguments and return values.

Everyone is encouraged to begin doing this for all new and modified code.  In the long run it should make the code easier to read and maintain.

Note: If you use type hinting for function/method arguments and/or return values, then **just** use type hinting.  Don't put the type in a docstring comment if you are using type hinting.  With tools like the [mypy](http://mypy-lang.org) static analysis type checker and [PyCharm](https://www.jetbrains.com/help/pycharm/type-hinting-in-product.html), it is much easier to ensure that Python 3 optional type hints stay in sync with actual code changes.  It is much more likely that the documentation comments could get out of sync.

issue_title: [RFC] Refactor `ratio`
issue_body:
`ratio` is still a bit of a weird parameters. Here is a proposal for further development:

* remove the deprecation regarding `float` as `ratio` which is a valid parameter with `binary` target.
* `'all'` in over-sampling should be changed to `'not majority'`. This is currently doing the same process but this is weird for the user.
* cleaning methods should have their own `ratio` type. `str` should be accepted but `dict` should be replaced by a `list` since we cannot select how many sample to select. Also `float` would not be accepted for the exact same reason.

`ratio` should also be renamed. My proposal `sampling_target`?

Well need to update how we send blueprint to AYS. Instead of sening the json serialized form of the blueprint, we should pass the yaml content as a string.

This is due to the fact that blueprint need to be ordered and json doesn't ensure order during serialization.

issue_title: Must have VPN to connect my web
Feature request: certbot-dns-gandi
issue_body:
The existing Gandi plugin at https://github.com/Gandi/letsencrypt-gandi only supports their v4 API and is deprecated and archived, with Gandi claiming it's not needed because they offer free SSL certificates with their hosting package. This leaves people hosting elsewhere but using Gandi DNS in the cold when it comes to setting up certbot to automate DNS verification.

However, Gandi now has a v5 API (docs at http://doc.livedns.gandi.net) and it would be great if certbot could update its DNS entries through this.

(Sadly, my Python is not currently up to writing the plugin myself and submitting a PR :)

Thanks!

issue_title: acme library crashes processing Unicode strings in ACME error detail
issue_labels: [{'id': 277074172, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20acme', 'name': 'area: acme', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 272789317, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20error%20handling', 'name': 'area: error handling', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 146954787, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/bug', 'name': 'bug', 'color': 'fc2929', 'default': True}]
issue_body:
Based on https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/unicodeencodeerror-running-the-certbot/32422, I investigated a bit and concluded that the `acme` library is unsafe when the CA returns an error detail string containing non-ASCII characters; it crashes with a `UnicodeEncodeError` at

https://github.com/certbot/certbot/blob/master/acme/acme/messages.py#L39

(Imagine that `err` is `u"Errör message"`; calling `str()` on that is an error.)

In this case, a user had a 404 page containing Russian text and Certbot appeared to crash at the line above when trying to report a failed `webroot` authentication. I'm not certain that this is the only thing preventing Certbot from handling such an error properly, but if I've understood the problem properly, it's at least one obstacle.

issue_title: `acme` module's `client._revoke` sends incorrect `ContentType` header.
issue_body:
[ACME draft-10, section 6.2](https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-ietf-acme-acme-10#section-6.2) explicitly requires POSTs be sent with a `Content-Type` of `application/jose+json`: 

> Because client requests in ACME carry JWS objects in the Flattened
> JSON Serialization, they must have the "Content-Type" header field
> set to "application/jose+json".  If a request does not meet this
> requirement, then the server MUST return a response with status code
> 415 (Unsupported Media Type).

The good news is that Certbot & the `acme` module already do this in 99.99% of cases :tada: :+1: :balloon: 

The bad news is that `client._revoke` overrides the default (correct) `ContentType` with `none`: https://github.com/certbot/certbot/blob/77fdb4d7d6194989dcc775f2e0ad81b6147c2359/acme/acme/client.py#L231

This will make revocation requests fail in a world where this restriction is enforced (See https://github.com/letsencrypt/boulder/pull/3532) 

There doesn't seem to be a reason to want to override `ContentType` in this case so I believe the fix is to change `_revoke` to use the default.

Thanks!

issue_title: Use a eth_sign and EIP191 compatible signature format
issue_body:
Implemented in the smart contracts here: https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden-contracts/pull/252

Compatibility with EIP191 & `eth_sign`:

```
\x19Ethereum Signed Message:\n" + len(message) + message
message = <token_network_address> <chain_id> <message_type_id> <message specific data to sign>
```

## Implementation in the smart contracts

```
string public constant signature_prefix = '\x19Ethereum Signed Message:\n';
```

Message content is tightly packed as described here: https://solidity.readthedocs.io/en/v0.4.24/abi-spec.html#abi-packed-mode
Python tests implementation: https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden-contracts/pull/252/commits/1569bb635b178c9683c9714b0c92dff4899b4afd#diff-6180c16583e73c52c0366184d36015f9 (I would have used `encode_abi_packed` from https://eth-abi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/encoding.html#non-standard-packed-mode-encoding, but there are some dependencies issues at the moment)

### Balance Proof Message:

```js
        uint256 message_type_id = 1;

        // Length of the actual message: 20 + 32 + 32 + 32 + 32 + 32 + 32
        string memory message_length = '212';

        bytes32 message_hash = keccak256(abi.encodePacked(
            signature_prefix,  // string
            message_length,  // string
            token_network_address,  // address
            chain_id,  // uint256
            message_type_id,  // uint256
            channel_identifier,  // uint256
            balance_hash,  // bytes32
            nonce,  // uint256
            additional_hash  // bytes32
        ));
```

### Balance Proof Update Message:

```
        uint256 message_type_id = 2;

        // Length of the actual message: 20 + 32 + 32 + 32 + 32 + 32 + 32 + 65
        string memory message_length = '277';

        bytes32 message_hash = keccak256(abi.encodePacked(
            signature_prefix,  // string
            message_length,  // string
            token_network_address,  // address
            chain_id,  // uint256
            message_type_id,  // uint256
            channel_identifier,  // uint256
            balance_hash,  // bytes32
            nonce,  // uint256
            additional_hash  // bytes32
            closing_signature  // bytes
        ));
```

### Withdraw Message:

```
        uint256 message_type_id = 3;

        // Length of the actual message: 20 + 32 + 32 + 32 + 20 + 32
        string memory message_length = '168';

        bytes32 message_hash = keccak256(abi.encodePacked(
            signature_prefix,  // string
            message_length,  // string
            token_network_address,  // address
            chain_id,  // uint256
            message_type_id,  // uint256
            channel_identifier,  // uint256
            participant,  // address
            total_withdraw  // uint256
        ));
```

### Cooperative Settle Message:

```
        uint256 message_type_id = 4;

        // Length of the actual message: 20 + 32 + 32 + 32 + 20 + 32 + 20 + 32
        string memory message_length = '220';

        bytes32 message_hash = keccak256(abi.encodePacked(
            signature_prefix,  // string
            message_length,  // string
            token_network_address,  // address
            chain_id,  // uint256
            message_type_id,  // uint256
            channel_identifier,  // uint256
            participant1,  // address
            participant1_balance,  // uint256
            participant2,  // address
            participant2_balance  // uint256
        ));
```

issue_title: Install channel filters using from_block equal to the channel creation.
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

New channels are registered by a restart may be needed for a node to see a deposit.

My assumption is that there is a race condition, and the deposit happens faster than we install the filter, so the event can be missed.

Here is the code execute when a new channel is created:

- [ContractReceiveNewChannel](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/master/raiden/event_handler.py#L207-L208`) is handled, since the channel appears in the channels list
- As part of the new channel event [the channel is registered](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/3f8758c85fa84bbf0ae8ca68d2ae8990dde60a5e/raiden/event_handler.py#L245-L248)
- During channel registration [a filter is installed](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/3f8758c85fa84bbf0ae8ca68d2ae8990dde60a5e/raiden/raiden_service.py#L794), this was suppose to get the deposit events.

## Solution

Install the filter during channel registration using the `from_block` equal to the creation block of the channel.

## Tasklist

- [ ] Install the filter using the `from_block`  equal to the block in which the channel was created

issue_title: DDD Energy interpolation idea.
issue_body:
Im not sure if you need this but i thought that it might be of interest so here goes:

Since there was no energy interpolation function implemented in the DDD i took it upon myself to create a function that as of now is working for the purposes i need it to.
I have copied the original DDD.load_ddd(directory) and changed it to generate simply a list of energies and a dictionary of the ddd data but including FWHM 1 & 2 and the factor, as such:

```
def load_ddd(self, directory):
        x_data = []
        y_data = []
        z_data = []
        a_data = []
        b_data = []
        c_data = []
        points = [[], []]
        ddd = {}
        max_dist = []
        items = glob.glob(directory)
        for item in items:
            x_data = []
            y_data = []
            z_data = []
            a_data = []
            b_data = []
            with open(item, 'r') as f:
                data = f.read()
            lines = data.split('\n')
            for n, line in enumerate(data.split("\n")):
                if line.find("energy") is not -1:
                    energy = float(line.split()[1])
                    c_data.append(energy)
                if line.find('!') is -1 and line.find('#') is -1:
                    break
            for i in range(n, len(lines)):
                if len(lines[i]) < 3:
                    continue
                point = [float(s) for s in lines[i].split()]
                x_data.append(point[0])
                y_data.append(point[1])
                z_data.append(point[2])
                a_data.append(point[3])
                b_data.append(point[4])

            
            ddd[energy] = np.array([x_data, y_data, z_data, a_data, b_data]).T
            
        self.ddd = ddd
        self.energy = np.asarray(c_data)
```

with that i then created a function called DDD.Generate_interpolated_DDD(energy), that creates a part of the same self.ddd directory but with a energy that the user defines, with simple **linear** interpolation, as such:

```
def Generate_interpolated_DDD(self, energy):
        nearest_energy = self.energy[np.abs(self.energy-energy).argmin()]
        
        if nearest_energy-energy >= 0:
            try:
                nearest_energy = self.energy[np.abs(self.energy-energy).argmin()-1]
            except:
                print('Energy range of .ddd files inadequate')      
        try:
            nearest_energy_2 = self.energy[np.abs(self.energy-energy).argmin()+1]
        except:
            print('Energy range of .ddd files inadequate')
        
        ddd_1 = self.ddd[nearest_energy]
        ddd_2 = self.ddd[nearest_energy_2]

        range_1 = ddd_1[np.where(ddd_1 == np.max(ddd_1[:,2]))[0], 0]
        range_2 = ddd_2[np.where(ddd_2 == np.max(ddd_2[:,2]))[0], 0]
        scaling_factor = range_2/range_1
        lista=[]
        
        depths = ddd_1[:,0]
        
        dist = ddd_2[:,0]*(scaling_factor*133/140)
        dist = dist[dist < ddd_2[-1,0]]
        for dist in dist:
            nearest_dist_nr = np.abs(ddd_1[:, 0]*scaling_factor-dist).argmin()
            nearest_dist_2_nr = np.abs(ddd_2[:, 0]-dist).argmin()
            
            if ddd_1[nearest_dist_nr, 0]*scaling_factor-dist <= 0:
                nearest_dist_nr -= 1
            
            if ddd_2[nearest_dist_2_nr, 0]-dist <= 0:
                nearest_dist_2_nr -= 1
                
            if nearest_dist_nr == len(ddd_1[:,0])-1:
                nearest_dist_nr -= 1
                
            if nearest_dist_2_nr == len(ddd_2[:,0])-1:
                nearest_dist_2_nr -= 1
                
            temp_ddd_1 = []
            temp_ddd_2 = []
            for number in range(1, 5):
                temp_ddd_1.append(azz.interpolate(dist/scaling_factor ,ddd_1[nearest_dist_nr, 0] ,ddd_1[nearest_dist_nr+1, 0], ddd_1[nearest_dist_nr, number], ddd_1[nearest_dist_nr+1, number]))
                temp_ddd_2.append(azz.interpolate(dist ,ddd_2[nearest_dist_2_nr, 0] ,ddd_2[nearest_dist_2_nr+1, 0], ddd_2[nearest_dist_2_nr, number], ddd_2[nearest_dist_2_nr+1, number]))
            
            lista.append([float(dist/(scaling_factor*133/140)), azz.interpolate(energy, 130, 140, temp_ddd_1[0], temp_ddd_2[0]), azz.interpolate(energy, 130, 140, temp_ddd_1[1], temp_ddd_2[1]), azz.interpolate(energy, 130, 140, temp_ddd_1[2], temp_ddd_2[2]), azz.interpolate(energy, 130, 140, temp_ddd_1[3], temp_ddd_2[3])])

        lista = np.array(lista)
        lista[np.where(lista[:,1] < 0), 1] = 0
        lista[np.where(lista[:,2] < 0), 2] = 0
        lista[np.where(lista[:,3] < 0), 3] = 0
        lista[np.where(lista[:,4] < 0), 4] = 0
        
        self.ddd[energy] = lista
```

the last part simply forces all negative values to 0.
So when running, whatever.Generate_interpolated_DDD(133), you can then call the DDD file using whatever.ddd[133] to get a interpolated DDD file for 133 MeV. I have plotted them and they look fine to me:
Cyan is 130 MeV
Magenta is 133 MeV
Yellow is 140 MeV.

issue_title: Definition of dryrun mode
issue_body:
Dryrun mode is very difficult to define because of the dynamic nature of sos. It currently,

1. does not report error is no input file is available, but the behavior is undefined if the input is provided by another step.

2. does not execute most actions (action return `None`).

3. does not verify output file

4. does not check or save signature

I am wondering if we should get rid of 1, because a DAG cannot be built correctly without 1. Then 3 would have to be a `touch` function to generate intended output (so satisfy the input of next step).

issue_title: Issue with FP16 with Sequential Models
issue_body:
The current implementation of `Learner.half()` modifies `Learner.models.model` to a new object, of type `FP16`. Currently this does not work with sequential models. For example, I tried to use `Learner.half()` on the `RNN_Learner` class for training a language model and ran into an issue when `get_layer_groups` was called:
```
1. class LanguageModel(BasicModel):
2.     def get_layer_groups(self):
3.         m = self.model[0]
4.         return [*zip(m.rnns, m.dropouths), (self.model[1], m.dropouti)]

```
The error I get is that objects of type `FP16`  (in this case it the object in question is `self.model` in line 3) cannot be indexed. Thus, by forcing the model to be of type `FP16` we lose any additional properties of the original `model` object that are not directly inherited by `nn.Module` (such as `_get_item_by_idx`). Is there a different approach to introducing fp16 into arbitrary models that could solve this issue?

issue_title: Ray with docker: expose the jupyter UI
issue_body:
Hello all,

First, let me congratulate the whole team for this framework. This is **incredibly cool** and has empowered my research tremendously. It has a design philosophy that I love: only changes a few lines of the original sequential code and you are up and running. Now, onto my suggestion.

### System information
- Docker version 17.03.0-ce, build 60ccb22
- Ubuntu 16.04
- Ray 0.3.1
- Python 3.5.2

### Exposing Jupyter UI

I am using ray to parallelize training of RL agents across multiple nodes in a cluster. Each of these nodes are running ray inside a docker container. Now the head node is also running the UI under `localhost:8888`. However, even with the correct port forwarding this is not reachable from the outside world. I had to modify of `ray/services.py`, [in the `start_ui` function, line 773](https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/6f8b81d9e521a9a4aeb59478c0d1844f08d6ab81/python/ray/services.py#L737):

```python
    command = [
        "jupyter", "notebook", "--no-browser", "--port={}".format(port),
        "--ip=0.0.0.0",
        "--NotebookApp.iopub_data_rate_limit=10000000000",
        "--NotebookApp.open_browser=False",
        "--NotebookApp.token={}".format(token)
]
```

After this modification, I was able to reach the notebook. However, if I start the kernel and run all cells, nothing would ever show up, but nothing fails either, so I do not really know how to go from there.

In any case, the fact that nothing shows up might be related to my setup. However, my suggestion is to either default to `--ip=0.0.0.0` or let the use chose which IP to expose.

Cheers,
Guillaume

issue_title: Release Improvements
issue_body:
The aim of this issue is to track the changes required to simplify the release process and make @cdrage's life easier, or allow others to perform releases.

I think the process should end up being three steps only, via a script or possibly the GitHub UI. 

1. Create PR with updated change log for the new version.
2. Merge PR.
3. Create tag for the new version. 

The rest of the process should be handled automatically, triggered by creating the tag. 
 
 - [x] Automatically add the Windows installer to GitHub release (#328)
 - [x] Update the release information using travis from the change log (#328)
 - [ ] Include README in the package long_description 
 - [ ] Automatically deploy the `streamlink` package to PyPI [**?**]
 - [ ] Automatically add the sdist package GitHub release [**?**]
 - [ ] Deploy a wheel to PyPI  [**?**]
 - [ ] stable/dev versions of the docs 
 - [ ] Make the change log consistently markdown, and include it in the docs using `recommonmark`
 - [ ] Only include installation/open collective in GitHub release info. 

1. We can include the README.md file in the package metadata as long_description to improve the appearance of the `streamlink` package on PyPI. See https://pypi.org/project/streamlink/0.12.1/ to see how it looks right now.

1. We _could_ deploy the `streamlink` package to PyPI automatically when creating a release tag, this would streamline the release process and remove some of the manual work and possibility for mistakes.

1. We _could_ add the `sdist` package to the GitHub release automatically with the release tag, as we do with the Windows installer. 

1. We _could_ also deploy a wheel to PyPI either automatically or manually, depending on what we do with the others. This might speed up installations in some cases, and is the preferred binary distribution method - but each platform variant might need its own wheel.

1. Separate the docs in to 2 streams (dev and stable), where stable is the default page. A link to the dev version will also need to be added a long with a note stating that is it the development/nightly version. Possibly we could deploy the dev docs at the same time as the nightlies are built, and deploy the stable version on release tag. 

1. The change log is currently a mix of reStructuredText and Markdown, it should be Markdown only. It should be able to be included in the docs by using `recommonmark` in Sphinx to render the Markdown. 

1. The installation instructions and open collective info don't need to be included for every single version in the release log, they are useful in the GitHub releases though. The script that updates the GitHub releases can be modified to include this template, then it doesn't need to be included in the change log. 

As always, it would be nice to have some feedback from the rest of you! I'm sure there are some other things we can improve 😄

Likewise, the text boxes should have an option to only update the value when the text box is blurred, or maybe when the user presses enter in a one-line textbox (sort of the equivalent of the on_submit handler textboxes currently have).

issue_title: Flickering or jumpy interacts
issue_body:
In #1494, @JazzTap pointed out that interact examples were flickering and jumpy. I've been looking into this more:

1. There is an optimization in the classic notebook output areas where if you clear the output with the `wait=True` argument, it will fix the height of the output area until the next new output arrives. I thought maybe we weren't triggering that feature, but I checked and we are.
2. The output widget is suffering from a problem in how we check for what messages to send back to the server. In the default `from_json` at https://github.com/jupyter-widgets/ipywidgets/blob/4ffa003d18e54d14d1c33dcc71b4282f7fa60996/ipywidgets/widgets/widget.py#L650, we just return the object. However, since outputs are in a *tuple*, but the incoming json is a *list*, the comparison at https://github.com/jupyter-widgets/ipywidgets/blob/4ffa003d18e54d14d1c33dcc71b4282f7fa60996/ipywidgets/widgets/widget.py#L599 always fails. The problem is that the roundtrip from python -> json -> python changes tuples into lists. I'll put an issue for this, but likely we'll need to do a conversion round trip as the default (hopefully this isn't too slow...). Basically, this problem means that the output is cleared and reset multiple times, causing flicker. This is fixed in #1523.
3. Even fixing the above issue, we have jitter. This comes from a problem in the feature above in (1): https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/issues/2668
4. Even fixing all of the above problems, we'll still have an issue with the image manipulation interact example right now since there is a deprecation warning displayed. Since the height is only fixed until the first new input comes in, and that first new input is the deprecation warning, the interact output height is still reset and you still see a flicker where the image is. To solve this, get rid of the warning so that the first output is the image. We'd have the same flicker problem if there was a legitimate piece of text printed above the image as well. To alleviate this flicker, we might introduce a slight delay (10ms?) in clearing the height in the notebook's output area. That might be worth exploring as a notebook PR in the future. The interact error itself is fixed in #1524.

issue_title: Allow controlling readout width in Valid widget
issue_body:
The `Valid` widget does not provide a way to control the `readout` style, as far as I can tell. This was originally raised in issue #1937 .

One way to do this would be to create a new `ValidStyle` object for the `Valid` widget that contains both the `description_width` and the `readout_width` fields. The `ValidStyle` object should probably inherit from `DescriptionStyle`.

Alternatively, we could make a new `ReadoutStyle` widget, with a corresponding `readout_style` traitlet on the `Valid` widget. This seems a bit more extensible in the future.

issue_title: DSSClient spontaneaously reverts to prod, even when configured with different host
issue_body:
The [prescribed](https://github.com/HumanCellAtlas/dcp-cli/blame/17e1b202f81e4fa1a0306e417ca528bbd7d150c4/README.rst#L54) method to switch the DSSClient to a different host is 

```
client = hca.dss.DSSClient()
client.host = "https://dss.example.com/v1"
```

There are two problems with this approach:

For one the swagger def is downloaded (or loaded from disk) in the constructor, defaulting to the HCA prod instance. There is no guarantee that the `dss.example.com` endpoint has a compatible API.

More puzzling is that the client seems to sporadically revert to using the prod instance, even when configured to a different host as outlined above. This must be a race condition. I do have multiple threads sharing a client.

If I instantiate the client in the following way, the problem goes away (the client does not revert to prod):

```
 config = HCAConfig("hca")
 config['DSSClient'].swagger_url = 'https://dss.example.com/v1/swagger.json'
 self.dss_client = DSSClient(config=config)
```

This is all I got, I don't have time to dig deeper right now.

issue_title: Add aggregatable fields and index name to CSV Schema definition
issue_body:
[Moved from https://github.com/chaoss/grimoirelab-sigils/issues/144]

From https://github.com/chaoss/grimoirelab-sigils/issues/117:
>* CSV files needs a way to define when text fields should be aggregatable. By default all fields are aggregatable by default except text fields. I added a workaround considering that in our current CSVs, text implies being aggregatable. This should be removed when CSVs are improved.
>* CSV files are not specifying index names. As a workaround, I used file name without file extension for this.

issue_title: Convolution/ Cross-correlation?
issue_body:
At this stage, it isn't possible to decide if the `Conv` operator does Convolutions (like Keras/TensorFlow) or Cross-correlation (like PyTorch). The Conv node test has the weight kernel consisting of all 1's , hence rendering it of no real use for inferring the nature of the operation.

Can someone tell me which operation the `Conv` operator does?
Also, would it be possible to upload better Conv tests, (probably having a kernel with not all values same?)

LSTM operator - can't get final C state

issue_title: 当‘geo_cities_coords’生效时，Geolines的多例模式出现部分参数失效
issue_body:

-->

**问题**
当‘geo_cities_coords’生效时，Geolines的多例模式的label_color字段失效,并且图例的个数也不正确


**运行环境（系统环境及 pyecharts 版本）**
 window10+python 3.6.3



**代码及截图**

`def geolines_demo():
    style = Style(
        title_top="#fff",
        title_pos="center",
        width=1200,
        height=600,
        background_color="#404a59"
    )
    style_geo = style.add(
        is_label_show=True,
        line_curve=0.2,
        line_opacity=0.6,
        legend_text_color="#eee",
        legend_pos="right",
        geo_effect_symbol="plane",
        geo_effect_symbolsize=15,
        geo_effect_color="#123456",
        geo_cities_coords={"自定义1": [125.260335, 43.806909], "自定义2": [114.267795, 30.375808]},
        label_color=['#a6c84c', '#ffa022', '#46bee9'],
        label_pos="right",
        label_formatter="{b}",
        label_text_color="#eee"
    )
    custom_1 = [
        ["自定义1", "上海", 10],
        ["自定义1", "北京", 20],
        ["自定义1", "南京", 30],
        ["自定义1", "重庆", 40],
        ["自定义1", "兰州", 50],
        ["自定义1", "杭州", 60],
    ]
    custom_2 = [
        ["自定义2", "无锡"],
        ["自定义2", "合肥"],
        ["自定义2", "南京"],
        ["自定义2", '西安'],
        ["自定义2", "大理"],
        ["自定义2", "郑州"]
    ]

    geolines = GeoLines("GeoLines 示例", **style.init_style)
    geolines.add("custom_1", custom_1, **style_geo)
    geolines.add("custom_2", custom_2, **style_geo)
    geolines.render("geoline_demo.html")
`
